title: llmledger JSONL log record
description: One line of an llmledger log file is exactly one JSON object matching this schema. This is the source-of-truth contract for any client (Python or otherwise, e.g. Node.js/Go agents) that wants to write logs compatible with `llmledger report`/`detect`.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record was written against, e.g. "1.0". Allows future format changes without breaking older readers.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp the call was logged, ISO 8601.

label.description: User-chosen name for this call site / step (e.g. "retrieval", "summarize").

input_tokens.description: Input tokens billed at the standard (non-cached) input rate.

cached_input_tokens.description: Input tokens billed at the cheaper cached-input rate (prompt caching), counted separately from input_tokens.

cost_micros.description: Cost of this call in integer micros (1 micro = $0.000001), not float dollars. Whole cents are too coarse for per-token LLM pricing (a single call is often sub-cent); micros give six decimal digits of precision while remaining an exact integer, avoiding both per-call rounding loss and float-accumulation drift when aggregating a large log.

trace_id.description: Optional identifier grouping multiple related calls belonging to one logical agent request (e.g. retrieval + generation + tool-call).

extra.description: Optional free-form metadata passed by the caller (e.g. workflow_id, chunk_id). Long string fields (>200 chars) trigger a one-time content-leak warning.
